nodes.label: Pino Structured Logging
nodes.purpose: JSON-structured Pino logging with mandatory correlation fields; console.log forbidden in production code.
what_happens: Every log line emitted as JSON via Pino (REQ-OBS-001)
Mandatory correlation fields attached: audit_run_id, client_id, page_url, node_name, heuristic_id, trace_id (REQ-OBS-002)
Log levels used consistently: debug (dev only), info (state transitions), warn (degraded), error (recovered failures), fatal (audit cannot continue) (REQ-OBS-003)
Sensitive data redaction enforced: no heuristic content, no credentials, no full HTML/DOM, no PII (REQ-OBS-004)
console.log/console.error forbidden in production code (REQ-OBS-005)
inputs: LogContext { audit_run_id?, client_id?, page_url?, node_name?, heuristic_id?, trace_id? }
outputs: JSON log lines to stdout / log sink

internal_architecture.summary: Pino logger configured with mandatory correlation context. All production code uses this logger; console.log is forbidden by lint rule.

components.name: LogContext type
components.role: TypeScript interface enforcing correlation field shape

internal_flow: Code calls logger.info(context, message)
Pino formats as JSON with timestamp, level, correlation fields
Output to stdout for log aggregator ingestion
trace_id ties multi-node operations together

nodes.why_matters: You cannot operate what you cannot observe. Structured logs with correlation fields make audit failures debuggable instead of mysterious.
nodes.example: logger.info({ audit_run_id: 'a-123', page_url: 'https://shop.example.com/cart', node_name: 'evaluate', heuristic_id: 'H-cart-001' }, 'evaluation started')

nodes.label: audit_events Table
nodes.purpose: Append-only event log with 22 canonical AuditEventTypes streamed via SSE and retained 90 days.
what_happens: Every significant state transition emits an AuditEvent to audit_events table and via SSE stream (REQ-OBS-010)
AuditEvent schema: id, audit_run_id, client_id, event_type, page_url, metadata (event-specific), timestamp (REQ-OBS-011)

22 canonical event types: audit_started, audit_completed, audit_failed, page_browse_started/completed/failed, page_analyze_started/completed/skipped, finding_produced, finding_grounding_rejected, finding_critique_rejected, finding_published, budget_warning, budget_exceeded, llm_call_completed, llm_call_failed, llm_provider_fallback, perception_quality_low, hitl_requested, cross_page_analysis_completed, overlay_dismissed (REQ-OBS-012)
Append-only: no UPDATE, no DELETE; 90 days active retention then archived to cold storage (REQ-OBS-013)
All events emitted within a single graph node share the same trace_id for LangSmith correlation (REQ-OBS-014)
inputs: Event emissions from any graph node
outputs: AuditEvent rows persisted in audit_events table
SSE stream events for dashboard consumers
Input feed for Layer 3 derived metrics

internal_architecture.summary: Append-only event ledger with 22 typed events and structured metadata, feeding both real-time SSE and batch metrics computation.

components.name: Event emitter
components.role: Writes to DB + SSE stream atomically

internal_flow: Graph node emits an event with type + metadata
Emitter writes append-only row to audit_events
Same event broadcast on SSE for live consumers
Aged events (>90d) moved to cold storage

nodes.why_matters: Events are the substrate for dashboard rendering, post-hoc analysis, and metrics. Append-only guarantees auditability.
nodes.example: page_analyze_completed event: { page_url: '/checkout', findings_count: 7, duration_ms: 18450, cost_usd: 0.42 }

nodes.label: Layer 3 Derived Metrics
nodes.purpose: 8 audit-level + 8 heuristic-level metrics computed from events and llm_call_log for trending and quality monitoring.
what_happens: Audit-level metrics computed from audit_events + llm_call_log + findings (REQ-OBS-020)
8 audit-level metrics: duration_seconds, actual_cost_usd, cost_vs_estimate_ratio, pages_complete_rate, pages_skipped_count, findings_per_page_avg, grounding_rejection_rate, cache_hit_rate

Heuristic-level HeuristicHealthMetrics per heuristic_id: total_evaluations, findings_produced, passes, grounding_rejections, critique_rejections, consultant_approvals, consultant_rejections, health_score, last_updated (REQ-OBS-021)
health_score formula: (produced - grounding_rej - consultant_rej) / total
Phase 9 implementation

internal_architecture.summary: SQL/materialized-view computations producing audit-scope and heuristic-scope metrics on top of append-only event and call-log tables.
internal_flow: Pull events + llm_call_log + findings rows for the audit
Apply 8 deterministic formulas (e.g., SUM cost_usd, COUNT skipped pages)
Per heuristic, aggregate counters and compute health_score

nodes.why_matters: Metrics turn raw events into operational insight: cost accuracy, reliability, finding quality, and per-heuristic health for IP curation.
nodes.example: Heuristic H-trust-007: total_evaluations=120, findings_produced=82, grounding_rejections=18, consultant_rejections=21, health_score=0.36

nodes.label: 7 Alert Rules
nodes.purpose: BullMQ scheduled job runs every 5 min checking 7 rules with debounced notifications via NotificationAdapter.
what_happens: BullMQ scheduled job runs every 5 minutes checking alert rules (REQ-OBS-030)

7 rules: audit_stuck_warning (>45min, warning), audit_stuck_critical (>90min, critical), grounding_rejection_high (>80% page rejected, warning), heuristic_health_low (health_score<0.3, warning), cost_overrun (actual>2x estimated, warning), llm_provider_errors (5+ errors/10min, critical), audit_failure_cluster (3+ failed/hour, critical)
Notifications delivered via NotificationAdapter (§14 v2.2a). Email for MVP. Webhook post-MVP (REQ-OBS-031)
Debounced: same alert type for the same audit_run_id fires at most once per hour (REQ-OBS-032)
outputs: Log entries for each fired rule
Optional pause prompt for critical audits

internal_architecture.summary: Scheduled rule evaluator that reads metrics + events, classifies severity, applies per-audit/per-type debouncing, and routes to notification channels.
internal_flow: Cron tick every 5 minutes
Each rule queries metrics/events for its condition
If condition met and not debounced, emit notification via NotificationAdapter
Record fire time for debouncing window

nodes.why_matters: Alerts surface degraded behavior before customers notice; debouncing prevents alert storms from a single failing audit.
nodes.example: audit_stuck_critical fires once after 90 min on audit a-456, sending email to admin with pause option; second event in same hour suppressed.

nodes.label: Operations Dashboard
nodes.purpose: Admin-only /console/admin/operations route showing active audits, summary, heuristic health, alerts, cost trend, failure breakdown.
what_happens: Admin route /console/admin/operations provides real-time operational visibility; access restricted to consultant role with admin flag (REQ-OBS-040)

6 dashboard sections: Active Audits (progress, ETA, cost burn), 24h Summary, Heuristic Health Table (sortable by health_score), Alert Feed (last 24h), Cost Trend (30-day chart), Failure Breakdown (pie chart) (REQ-OBS-041)
Read-only: queries audit_events, llm_call_log, materialized heuristic_health_metrics view; no live writes from dashboard (REQ-OBS-042)
Phase 9 last — built after consultant dashboard, client dashboard, PDF reports (§34.8)
outputs: 6 sections with charts and sortable tables

internal_architecture.summary: Read-only admin UI surfacing 6 operational sections backed by materialized views and event queries.

components.name: Active Audits panel
components.role: Live progress with ETA + cost burn

internal_flow: Admin authenticates via Clerk (admin flag required)
Dashboard SSR/CSR renders 6 panels
Each panel queries its data source (events / llm_call_log / view)
User sorts/filters; no writes performed

nodes.why_matters: Single pane of glass for ops staff to spot stuck audits, cost overruns, or systemic regressions before they cascade.
nodes.example: Admin opens /console/admin/operations and sees audit a-789 stuck for 60 min with $14 cost burn (warning state) and heuristic H-cta-014 trending health_score=0.28.

nodes.label: HeuristicHealthMetrics View
nodes.purpose: Materialized view of per-heuristic health; health_score < 0.3 flags rewrite candidate, feeding §28 Learning Service.
what_happens: Per-heuristic counters maintained: total_evaluations, findings_produced, passes, grounding_rejections, critique_rejections, consultant_approvals, consultant_rejections (REQ-OBS-021)
health_score = (produced - grounding_rej - consultant_rej) / total
health_score < 0.3 → heuristic flagged for rewrite (REQ-OBS-022)
Feeds §28 Learning Service for IP curation
inputs: Consultant approval/rejection signals from review workflow

internal_architecture.summary: Materialized view aggregating evaluation + rejection + approval counters per heuristic_id, deriving a single health_score with a 0.3 rewrite threshold.
internal_flow: Aggregate counters per heuristic_id over evaluation events
Compute health_score on view refresh
If score < 0.3, mark for rewrite
Feed §28 Learning Service queue

nodes.why_matters: Closes the loop: heuristics that consistently produce rejected findings are surfaced for human-led rewrite, protecting deliverable quality.
nodes.example: Heuristic H-trust-007 with health_score=0.28 (below 0.3 threshold) is flagged for rewrite and queued for §28 Learning Service.

nodes.label: ExecutiveSummary
nodes.purpose: Deterministic 0-100 score + grade A-F, top 5 findings, strengths, plus 1 LLM call for recommended_next_steps.
what_happens: audit_complete node produces ExecutiveSummary as typed output (REQ-REPORT-001)
Deterministic overall_score = 100 - (critical*15 + high*8 + medium*3 + low*1), clamped 0-100 (REQ-REPORT-002)
Grade thresholds: A>=85, B>=70, C>=55, D>=40, F<40
Strengths detection deterministic: heuristic passing on >=80% of applicable pages added to strengths; no LLM (REQ-REPORT-003)
recommended_next_steps: 1 LLM call, temperature=0, $0.10 budget cap, GR-007 grounding applied (no conversion predictions); input is top 5 + pattern findings (REQ-REPORT-004)
top_findings sort by priority score (§23.4), ties broken by severity then confidence_tier (REQ-REPORT-005)
inputs: audit metadata (pages_analyzed, pages_skipped, total_cost_usd, duration_seconds, heuristic_sources)
outputs: ExecutiveSummary { overall_score, overall_grade, severity counts, top_findings[5], category_breakdown, strengths[], patterns[], recommended_next_steps, audit_metadata }

internal_architecture.summary: Deterministic score + bucketing + strengths in pure code; one tightly-budgeted LLM call to draft 3-5 next-step sentences.

components.name: Strengths detector
components.role: Heuristics passing on >=80% of applicable pages

internal_flow: Count findings by severity, compute score + grade
Detect strengths from pass-rate per heuristic
Sort findings by priority for top_findings
Single LLM call producing recommended_next_steps under budget
Apply GR-007 final regex check on returned text

nodes.why_matters: The ExecSummary is what decision-makers actually read; deterministic scoring keeps it reproducible while one bounded LLM call adds narrative.
nodes.example: Audit with 2 critical, 5 high, 8 medium → score = 100 - (30 + 40 + 24) = 6 → grade F; top 5 sorted by priority; next_steps describes the dominant theme.

nodes.label: ActionPlan (4 quadrants)
nodes.purpose: Quick wins / strategic / incremental / deprioritized bucketing by impact x effort; deterministic, no LLM.
what_happens: audit_complete produces ActionPlan grouping findings into 4 quadrants: quick_wins, strategic, incremental, deprioritized (REQ-REPORT-010)
Each quadrant carries label, description, findings[], estimated_total_effort_hours, page_count
Bucketing logic deterministic, no LLM (REQ-REPORT-011): high_impact = business_impact >= 6, low_effort = effort_hours <= 8
Quadrant matrix: high_impact + low_effort = Quick Wins; high_impact + high_effort = Strategic; low_impact + low_effort = Incremental; low_impact + high_effort = Deprioritized
estimated_total_effort_hours sourced from heuristic effort_category through EFFORT_MAP (§23.4); pattern findings aggregate effort across affected pages
Within each quadrant, findings sort by priority descending (REQ-REPORT-012)
inputs: Pattern findings with affected page sets
outputs: ActionPlan { quick_wins, strategic, incremental, deprioritized } each as ActionPlanPhase

internal_architecture.summary: Pure-function bucketing on (business_impact, effort_hours) with priority-descending sort within each quadrant.

components.name: Effort aggregator
components.role: Sums effort across pages for patterns

internal_flow: For each grounded finding, compute (business_impact, effort_hours)
Map to quadrant via 2x2 matrix
Aggregate effort + page_count per quadrant
Sort findings within quadrant by priority desc

nodes.why_matters: Decision-makers and dev teams need a sequencing map, not a flat list; quadrants make 'what to fix first' explicit.
nodes.example: Finding 'missing trust badge on PDP' with business_impact=7, effort_hours=4 → Quick Wins; 'redesign checkout flow' impact=8, effort_hours=80 → Strategic.

nodes.label: ReportGenerator
nodes.purpose: Service in packages/agent-core/src/delivery/ReportGenerator.ts; renders Next.js HTML then Playwright page.pdf().
what_happens: ReportGenerator service in packages/agent-core/src/delivery/ReportGenerator.ts produces a branded PDF from ExecSummary + ActionPlan + grounded findings (REQ-REPORT-020)

Approach (REQ-REPORT-021): Next.js route /api/report/[audit_run_id]/render renders report-ready HTML; headless Playwright navigates with auth; page.pdf({ format: 'A4', printBackground: true }) converts to PDF; PDF uploaded to Cloudflare R2 at /{client_id}/reports/{audit_run_id}/report.pdf
No new dependencies — reuses Playwright + Next.js already in stack
Delivery (REQ-REPORT-025): downloadable from consultant + client dashboards; link stored in audit_runs.report_pdf_url; optionally emailed via NotificationAdapter on audit_completed event
Partial reports (REQ-REPORT-030): if any page has analysis_status != complete, append 'Pages Not Fully Analyzed' section with URL, status, reason, recommended manual action

internal_architecture.summary: HTML template route + Playwright PDF capture pipeline with R2 upload and DB pointer update.
internal_flow: Build HTML template populated with ExecSummary + ActionPlan + findings
Headless Playwright authenticates and navigates to route
page.pdf({ format: 'A4', printBackground: true }) captures PDF
Upload PDF to R2 path; update DB; optionally email

nodes.why_matters: PDF is the artifact that gets forwarded in email — it is the deliverable that justifies the engagement.
nodes.example: Audit a-123 finishes; ReportGenerator renders /api/report/a-123/render, captures PDF, uploads to R2 at /clientX/reports/a-123/report.pdf, sets audit_runs.report_pdf_url.

nodes.label: ReportTemplate
nodes.purpose: Per-client branding config: logo, colors, company_name, footer; falls back to REO Digital / Neural branding.
what_happens: Per-client ReportTemplate config: client_id, logo_url (R2 URL or data URI), primary_color (hex), secondary_color, company_name, footer_text (REQ-REPORT-023)
Fallback to REO Digital / Neural branding when client template is missing
outputs: Branding config consumed by ReportGenerator HTML template

internal_architecture.summary: Static per-client branding record loaded at report render time, with fallback to default Neural branding.

components.name: Loader with fallback
components.role: Returns default if client row missing

internal_flow: ReportGenerator looks up template by client_id
If missing, returns default Neural / REO Digital branding
Template values injected into HTML template (logo, colors, footer)

nodes.why_matters: Branded deliverables look like agency work product, not generic SaaS output; one config swap repaints the whole report.
nodes.example: Client 'Acme' has logo_url + primary_color #FF6600 + footer 'Confidential — Acme Inc.'; PDF renders with these instead of Neural defaults.

nodes.label: 8 PDF Sections + <5MB Rule
nodes.purpose: Cover, ExecSummary, ActionPlan, Findings by Category, Cross-Page Patterns, Funnel, Methodology, Appendix; <5MB target with JPEG70%, 50 crops max, font subsetting.
what_happens: 8 sections in order (REQ-REPORT-022): Cover Page (logo, audit date, site URL, grade badge, branding); Executive Summary (score, top 5, strengths, category chart, next steps); Action Plan (4 quadrants); Findings by Category (observation, assessment, severity badge, benchmark, annotated screenshot, recommendation); Cross-Page Patterns; Funnel Analysis (if funnel findings exist); Methodology Note; Appendix (full finding table, perception quality summary, pages skipped)

Size + quality budgets (REQ-REPORT-024): target < 5MB per PDF; screenshots compressed to JPEG 70%; max 50 annotated screenshot crops per report (one per finding, prioritized by severity); font embedding subsets Inter (primary) + JetBrains Mono (code/data)
Only published findings included (cross-ref §24 Two-Store Pattern)
outputs: Rendered 8-section HTML feeding REP3 PDF capture
Asset budget enforcement (max 50 crops, JPEG70%, font subset)

internal_architecture.summary: Section-by-section HTML composer with strict asset budgets to keep PDF under 5MB and load quickly in email clients.

components.name: Asset budgeter
components.role: Caps crops at 50, compresses to JPEG70%

internal_flow: Compose Cover with branding + grade
Compose ExecSummary, ActionPlan, Findings by Category
Compose Cross-Page Patterns + Funnel (conditional)
Compose Methodology + Appendix
Apply asset budgets (50 crops max, JPEG70%, subset fonts)
Hand HTML to REP3 for PDF capture

nodes.why_matters: Email-friendly size + consistent structure means the PDF actually gets opened and read, not bounced or stalled.
nodes.example: Audit produces 73 findings — top 50 by severity get annotated crops; remaining 23 listed in appendix table only; final PDF lands at 4.2MB.

nodes.label: GoldenTestCase
nodes.purpose: Frozen perception + page_type + heuristics + expected_findings + expected_false_positives; JSON in test/golden/, treated as production artifact.
what_happens: Golden test case is a frozen input + expected output pair (REQ-GOLDEN-001)
Frozen inputs (never change): perception (full saved AnalyzePerception), page_type, business_type, filtered_heuristics (exact heuristics used at validation time)
Expected outputs: expected_findings[] with heuristic_id, status (violation|pass), severity, must_contain[] key phrases; expected_false_positives[] with heuristic_id + reason
Stored as individual JSON files in test/golden/GT-XXX.json, committed to git, treated as production artifacts (REQ-GOLDEN-002)
Bootstrapping: first 5 hand-crafted during Phase 1-5; engineer runs pipeline, consultant validates, exported via pnpm golden:capture (REQ-GOLDEN-010)
Targets: Week 4 → 5 tests; Phase 7 end → 10; Phase 8 end → 15; Phase 9 end → 20 baseline coverage (REQ-GOLDEN-011)

internal_architecture.summary: JSON artifact pinning a perception + heuristic set + consultant-validated expected/false-positive findings; immutable and git-tracked.

components.name: GoldenTestCase interface
components.role: Frozen schema with id, source_url, captured_at, validated_by, perception, expected_findings, expected_false_positives

internal_flow: Engineer runs pipeline against real site
Consultant marks findings valid / invalid / missed
pnpm golden:capture exports as JSON
Consultant adds expected_false_positives
JSON committed to repo

nodes.why_matters: Quality regression net for heuristic + prompt changes; without it, quality degradation is silent and cumulative.
nodes.example: GT-001 'Amazon PDP — missing size guide': frozen perception, expected_findings includes H-pdp-size-guide violation with must_contain=['size chart','fit guide'], expected_false_positives excludes H-pdp-shipping.

nodes.label: MockBrowserEngine
nodes.purpose: Implements BrowserEngine returning saved perceptions + screenshots from test/fixtures/sites/; no Playwright, no network.
what_happens: MockBrowserEngine implements BrowserEngine interface (REQ-GOLDEN-041)
Returns saved HTML snapshots from test/fixtures/sites/
page_analyze() returns saved AnalyzePerception JSON
Screenshots return saved JPEG files
No Playwright, no network
Activated when NEURAL_MODE=offline (REQ-GOLDEN-040)
Fixture directory structure: test/fixtures/sites/{site}/ with perception.json, page-state.json, viewport.jpg, fullpage.jpg (REQ-GOLDEN-042)
CLI: pnpm fixture:capture --url <url> navigates real URL once and saves perception + screenshots reusable forever offline (REQ-GOLDEN-043)
inputs: Page URL (matched against fixture site key)

internal_architecture.summary: Drop-in BrowserEngine that reads pre-captured fixtures from disk instead of driving Playwright, enabling network-free testing.
internal_flow: Test invokes pipeline with NEURAL_MODE=offline
MockBrowserEngine looks up fixture by URL/site key
Returns saved AnalyzePerception + screenshots
Pipeline runs as if browser had executed

nodes.why_matters: Eliminates network flakiness and cost from CI; pipeline tests become deterministic at zero API spend.
nodes.example: Test runs pipeline against 'amazon-pdp' fixture; MockBrowserEngine returns perception.json + viewport.jpg from disk in milliseconds.

nodes.label: MockLLMAdapter
nodes.purpose: Implements LLMAdapter returning cached responses keyed by golden-test perceptions; updated nightly; no API calls.
what_happens: MockLLMAdapter implements LLMAdapter interface (REQ-GOLDEN-041)
For known inputs (matching golden test perceptions): returns cached responses
For unknown inputs: returns structured 'mock finding' with placeholder data
Tracks call count, simulated tokens, simulated cost
No Anthropic / OpenAI API calls
Cache directory: test/fixtures/llm-responses/ (e.g. evaluate-amazon-pdp.json, critique-amazon-pdp.json) (REQ-GOLDEN-042)
Cached responses are pre-captured per golden test from last nightly run (REQ-GOLDEN-030)
outputs: Mock structured response for unknown inputs

internal_architecture.summary: LLMAdapter surface backed by a hash-keyed cache of nightly-captured responses, falling back to mock data for unseen inputs.

components.name: Response cache loader
components.role: Reads JSON files for known perceptions

components.name: Mock generator
components.role: Returns placeholder finding for unknown inputs

internal_flow: Pipeline calls LLM with perception + prompt
MockLLMAdapter computes cache key from perception/prompt
If cache hit, return saved response
If miss, return structured mock finding
Increment simulated call count + cost

nodes.why_matters: Lets every PR run the full pipeline in <30s with zero API spend, while nightly real-LLM runs refresh the cache.
nodes.example: Fast-mode CI runs GT-001 → MockLLMAdapter loads test/fixtures/llm-responses/evaluate-amazon-pdp.json and returns cached evaluate output instantly.

nodes.label: Fast Mode (every PR)
nodes.purpose: MockLLM-backed run in PR CI: tests grounding/filtering/scoring/integration; ~30s; $0; catches deterministic bugs.
what_happens: Fast mode (every PR) uses MockLLMAdapter with cached responses (REQ-GOLDEN-030)
Tests grounding, heuristic filtering, scoring, deterministic pipeline logic
LLM responses pre-captured per golden test from last nightly run
Zero API cost
Runs in ~30 seconds
Catches: grounding rule bugs, filtering bugs, scoring bugs, integration bugs
CI triggers on PRs touching analysis/, heuristics/, heuristics-repo/, prompt templates (REQ-GOLDEN-020)
Pass criteria (REQ-GOLDEN-022): aggregate TP rate >=80%, FP rate <=20%, no individual test below 60% TP; unexpected findings logged but do not fail CI
CLI: pnpm test:golden runs fast mode (REQ-GOLDEN-043)

internal_architecture.summary: PR-time CI runner that executes the analyze pipeline against golden inputs with mocked LLM, computing TP/FP rates against pass criteria.
internal_flow: For each GoldenTestCase: load frozen inputs
Run evaluate (mocked LLM) → self_critique → grounding (deterministic 12 rules)
Compare outputs against expected_findings + expected_false_positives
Aggregate TP/FP rates; check pass criteria
Emit CI result

nodes.why_matters: Catches deterministic regressions (grounding/filtering/scoring) on every PR without burning API budget.
nodes.example: PR modifies GR-007 logic; fast mode runs 20 golden tests in 30s using cached LLM responses, finds 1 regression where GT-005 TP rate drops below 60%, blocks merge.

nodes.label: Nightly Mode (real LLM)
nodes.purpose: Scheduled 2am UTC real-LLM run; $1-2/run; >10% drop vs 7-day rolling average fires P1, blocks deployment.
what_happens: Nightly mode (scheduled 2am UTC) uses real LLM calls (REQ-GOLDEN-031)
Tests actual evaluate + critique prompt quality
Updates cached responses for fast mode
Costs ~$1-2 per run (20 tests × ~$0.10 each)
Catches: prompt quality regressions, model behavior drift, heuristic-prompt alignment issues
Regression alerting (REQ-GOLDEN-032): nightly scores drop > 10% vs 7-day rolling average → P1 alert via §34 observability; blocks next deployment until investigated
CLI: pnpm test:golden:nightly (manual trigger) or scheduled job (REQ-GOLDEN-043)
Quality gate philosophy (REQ-GOLDEN-051): regression safety net, NOT a substitute for unit/integration/type tests, NOT a development blocker
outputs: Refreshed test/fixtures/llm-responses/ cache for fast mode
P1 alert via §34 observability on >10% drop

internal_architecture.summary: Scheduled real-LLM run of all golden tests that refreshes the fast-mode cache and watches for prompt/model drift via 7-day rolling baseline.

components.name: Drift detector
components.role: Compares aggregate score to 7-day rolling avg

internal_flow: 2am UTC: run all golden tests with real LLM
Compute aggregate TP/FP
Save responses to fast-mode cache
Compare to 7-day rolling baseline
If drop > 10%, fire P1 alert and block next deployment

nodes.why_matters: Catches prompt-quality regressions and model drift that fast mode cannot see, while keeping fast-mode cache fresh.
nodes.example: Sonnet model update silently changes critique behavior; nightly TP rate drops from 84% to 71% (>10% drop), P1 alert fires, deployment blocked until prompt or fixtures investigated.

nodes.label: ContextProfile
nodes.purpose: Immutable 5-dimension profile with per-field {value, source, confidence}, hash for §25 reproducibility.
what_happens: ContextProfile is the typed output: meta (captured_at, capture_method, user_provided_fields[], inferred_fields[], overall_confidence, perception_layer_version), 5 dimensions, open_questions[], hash (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-* shape)
Each dimension field carries { value, source: ContextSource, confidence: number }
Immutable after capture (Object.freeze); consumers needing derived views produce a new artifact (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-003)
hash = SHA-256 of profile content for §25 reproducibility integration (REQ-CONTEXT-REPRO-001)
Pinned into ReproducibilitySnapshot via context_profile_id, context_profile_hash, context_capture_layer_version

internal_architecture.summary: Frozen typed object with 5 dimensions, per-field provenance, open questions, and a content hash for reproducibility.
internal_flow: Pipeline assembles dimension values + provenance
Open questions emitted for low-confidence fields
Profile hashed and Object.frozen
Persisted in context_profiles table; pinned in ReproducibilitySnapshot

nodes.why_matters: Every audit decision now traces back to context with explicit provenance — no silent assumptions, fully reproducible.
nodes.example: ContextProfile for /pricing page: business.archetype = { value: 'SaaS', source: 'schema_org', confidence: 0.92 }; goal.primary_kpi = { value: 'trial_start', source: 'user', confidence: 1 }; hash = sha256:abc123...

nodes.label: 6 ContextSources
nodes.purpose: Per-field provenance enum: user, url_pattern, schema_org, copy_inference, layout_inference, default.
what_happens: ContextSource enum identifies origin of every field value (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-001 implicit shape)

Six values: user (explicit intake), url_pattern (URL parsing), schema_org (JSON-LD/Schema.org parsing), copy_inference (CTA/copy patterns), layout_inference (HTML structure heuristics), default (enum default with confidence 0)
Recorded per field in dimension subtrees alongside value + confidence
MUST NOT silently guess (Constitution R25 / REQ-CONTEXT-BOUND-001 §3): every inference records source + confidence
outputs: ContextSource value attached to each ContextProfile field

internal_architecture.summary: Type-level enum embedded in every dimension field record, enabling auditing of how each context value was determined.
internal_flow: Each inference rule emits (value, source, confidence)
Writer attaches all three to dimension field
meta dot-paths track which fields came from user vs inference

nodes.why_matters: Provenance lets consultants instantly tell which context values were entered vs inferred, controlling trust appropriately.
nodes.example: page.type = { value: 'PDP', source: 'url_pattern', confidence: 0.95 } vs goal.primary_kpi = { value: 'purchase', source: 'user', confidence: 1 }.

nodes.label: 5 Dimensions Detail
nodes.purpose: Business / Page / Audience / Traffic / Goal — fields, enum domains, regulated-vertical constraint requirement, per-phase scope.
what_happens: Business dimension: archetype (D2C|marketplace|SaaS|subscription|B2B|enterprise|lead_gen|service), aov_tier (low|mid|high|enterprise), cadence (one_time|repeat|subscription|considered|contract), vertical (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-BUSINESS-001)

Page dimension: type (home|PLP|PDP|cart|checkout|post_purchase|category|landing|blog|about|pricing|comparison), funnel_stage (awareness|consideration|decision|retention), job (educate|convert|reassure|upsell|recover|retain|qualify|route), is_indexed (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-PAGE-001)

Audience dimension: buyer (consumer|prosumer|SMB|mid_market|enterprise|technical|non_technical), awareness_level (Schwartz unaware→most_aware) (Phase 13b), decision_style (impulse|researched|committee|habitual) (Phase 13b), sophistication (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-AUDIENCE-001)

Traffic dimension: primary_sources[] {channel, share?, creative_or_message?}, device_priority (mobile|desktop|balanced), mobile_share, geo_primary (Phase 13b), locale_primary (Phase 13b) (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-TRAFFIC-001)
Goal dimension: primary_kpi (REQUIRED; REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-GOAL-002), secondary_kpis, current_baseline, target_lift, constraints { regulatory, accessibility (WCAG_AA|WCAG_AAA|none), brand, technical }
Constraints REQUIRED for regulated verticals (pharma, fintech, gambling, healthcare, legal); validator rejects missing regulatory list (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-GOAL-003)
outputs: Validation rejections for missing required fields

internal_architecture.summary: Five dimension subtrees with strict enum domains, per-field provenance, and Phase 4b vs Phase 13b scoping.
internal_flow: Each dimension assembled from intake + inference
Required fields validated (primary_kpi, regulatory constraints when vertical regulated)
Dimension subtrees attached to ContextProfile

nodes.why_matters: Dimensions are the substrate for heuristic selection (and Phase 13b weight modifiers) — context-aware analysis vs generic checklist.
nodes.example: B2B + checkout + high AOV → trust-badge heuristic weight modifier 1.8; D2C + PDP + low AOV → 0.6 (Phase 13b illustration).

nodes.label: Inference Pipeline Order
nodes.purpose: JSON-LD @type > pricing patterns > CTA copy > domain TLD > price magnitude. Single GET via undici, cheerio parse, robots.txt + 5s timeout.
what_happens: Inference signal ordering for business archetype (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-BUSINESS-001 ordered list): 1) JSON-LD @type, 2) pricing patterns (/mo, per user, free shipping, book a demo), 3) CTA copy (Add to cart, Request demo, Start free trial, Get a quote), 4) Domain TLD + naming, 5) Price points (≥$1K without ATC → considered or B2B)

Capture flow (§37.3): receive AuditRequest → parse URL (cheap, high confidence) → optional HTML fetch (cheerio, no JS) → run inference per dimension → identify low-confidence fields → build open_questions → halt if blocking → merge user answers → finalize

Fetch policy (REQ-CONTEXT-FLOW-001): single GET via undici/node-fetch (no Playwright); realistic User-Agent (per §11.1.1); respect robots.txt (REQ-SAFETY-005); 5s timeout; degrade to URL-only on failure; cache by URL+ETag for re-runs; emit CONTEXT_FETCH_FAILED warning on failure
HTML parse via cheerio or linkedom; no JSDOM, no JS execution (REQ-CONTEXT-FLOW-002)
JSON-LD parse via jsonld package extracting @type, name, offers, description
outputs: Per-dimension inferred values with source + confidence
CONTEXT_FETCH_FAILED warning when HTML fetch fails

internal_architecture.summary: Lightweight pre-perception extractor: URL parse, optional one-shot HTML fetch, cheerio + JSON-LD parse, then ordered inference rules per dimension.

components.name: Ordered inference rules
components.role: Schema > pricing > CTA > TLD > price magnitude

internal_flow: Parse URL for page-type candidate
Fetch HTML once (cache by URL+ETag)
Extract JSON-LD @type → highest-priority signal
Apply pricing-pattern, CTA-copy, TLD, and price-magnitude inferences in order
Emit (value, source, confidence) per dimension field
Degrade gracefully on fetch failure

nodes.why_matters: Strong signals (Schema.org) trump weak ones (TLD), and the layer never executes JS, keeping it cheap, safe, and reproducible.
nodes.example: Site with JSON-LD @type=SoftwareApplication beats a /shop subdomain hint; archetype resolved to SaaS with confidence 0.92 from schema_org.

nodes.label: Confidence Thresholds
nodes.purpose: ≥0.9 act; 0.6–0.9 use + flag in report; <0.6 emit as open_question (blocking when field is required).
what_happens: Confidence thresholds (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-001): >=0.9 act on the value, no clarification needed; 0.6-0.9 use the value but flag in final report; <0.6 emit as open_question (becomes blocking if field is required)
blocking: true on an open_question means analysis CANNOT proceed without an answer (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-002)
Audit halts at audit_setup and surfaces the question via CLI prompt (Phase 4b) or dashboard form (Phase 13b)
Tie-in with CC6 blocking gate

internal_architecture.summary: Three-band threshold classifier converting confidence numbers into action / flag / question outcomes.
internal_flow: Compute confidence per field
Classify into one of three bands
If <0.6, emit open_question; set blocking when required
If 0.6-0.9, mark for final-report flag
If >=0.9, proceed silently

nodes.why_matters: Confidence-driven gating turns 'silent guesses' into either acceptable defaults or explicit clarifications — no quietly wrong audits.
nodes.example: Inferred page.type=PDP confidence 0.55 → open_question 'Is this a product detail page?'; if blocking, audit halts at setup until answered.

nodes.label: Blocking Gate
nodes.purpose: Halts audit_setup on missing primary_kpi or required regulatory constraints; runs BEFORE perception per Constitution R25.
what_happens: primary_kpi REQUIRED (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-GOAL-002) — AuditRequest validation rejects audits without it

Constraints REQUIRED for regulated verticals (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-GOAL-003): validator checks business.vertical against regulated list (pharma, fintech, gambling, healthcare, legal); if matched, constraints.regulatory MUST be non-empty
blocking: true on any open_question halts analysis at audit_setup until answered (REQ-CONTEXT-OUT-002)
This layer runs BEFORE perception (no headless browser, no state graph) — gates the audit on context certainty (§37.0)

Architectural boundaries (REQ-CONTEXT-BOUND-001 / Constitution R25): MUST NOT run perception, MUST NOT make heuristic judgments, MUST NOT silently guess, MUST NOT skip clarification, MUST NOT mutate page state, MUST NOT assume traffic without user input
outputs: Halt signal at audit_setup (CLI prompt or dashboard form)

internal_architecture.summary: Pre-perception gate that rejects audits without primary_kpi or required regulatory constraints, and surfaces blocking open_questions before any analysis runs.
internal_flow: Run validator on AuditRequest + draft ContextProfile
If missing primary_kpi → reject
If regulated vertical and empty regulatory → reject
If any blocking open_question → halt, surface to user
On answer, merge into ContextProfile and proceed

nodes.why_matters: Forces context certainty up front so downstream perception/heuristic work isn't built on silent assumptions or compliance violations.
nodes.example: User triggers audit on /checkout but omits primary_kpi → audit_setup rejects; user adds primary_kpi='purchase', audit proceeds.

nodes.label: Phase 13b Additions
nodes.purpose: Schwartz awareness levels, message-match per traffic source, decision_style, geo+locale, weight modifiers; Constitution R25 MUST NOT list.
what_happens: Phase 13b additions to context capture (§37.8 phase mapping)
Schwartz awareness levels: unaware → problem_aware → solution_aware → product_aware → most_aware (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-AUDIENCE-001 Phase 13b)
Message-match field per traffic source: creative_or_message captures ad copy, email subject, referrer expectation (REQ-CONTEXT-DIM-TRAFFIC-001 Phase 13b) — highest-impact CRO context most audits miss
decision_style inference: impulse | researched | committee | habitual (Phase 13b)
is_indexed (SEO vs paid landing) — distinct heuristic sets
Geo + locale formal capture (geo_primary ISO country, locale_primary BCP-47) — EU GDPR / CA CCPA / BR LGPD binding
Heuristic weight modifiers (REQ-CONTEXT-DOWNSTREAM-002): heuristic.weight_for(ctx) = base_weight * business_modifier * page_modifier * goal_modifier — context-aware analysis vs generic checklist
Constitution R25 MUST NOT list (REQ-CONTEXT-BOUND-001): MUST NOT run perception, make heuristic judgments, silently guess, skip clarification, mutate page state, assume traffic without user input
Dashboard intake form (Phase 13b) replaces CLI prompt for non-CLI audits
Weight modifiers explicitly deferred from Phase 4b — calibration data needed first

internal_architecture.summary: Phase 13b extension layer that adds Schwartz awareness, message-match, decision style, geo/locale, and heuristic weight modifiers atop the Phase 4b baseline.
internal_flow: Awaits Phase 4b baseline + calibration data
Adds Phase 13b dimensions to ContextProfile
Computes heuristic weight modifiers from context
Surfaces dashboard intake form for non-CLI audits

nodes.why_matters: Turns generic findings into context-aware priorities — the same heuristic earns 1.8x weight on a B2B high-AOV checkout vs 0.6x on a D2C low-AOV PDP.
nodes.example: Trust-badge heuristic base_weight=0.5 → B2B + checkout + high AOV → modifier 1.8 → final 0.9; D2C + PDP + low AOV → modifier 0.6 → final 0.3.

nodes.label: FixGenerator
nodes.purpose: Future system that generates full HTML/CSS/JS variations across frameworks — extends VariationIdeaGenerator from DRAFT snippets to runnable code.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would extend VariationIdeaGenerator's DRAFT snippets into complete HTML/CSS/JS variations runnable in production
Named as a future interface in §01 system identity and §17 context preservation
inputs: Grounded finding + variation idea (DRAFT snippet) from §07/§17

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Closes the loop from finding to deployable code, dramatically reducing the engineering distance between recommendation and experiment.
nodes.example: Future: a finding 'CTA contrast too low' yields a complete CSS patch + JS hook ready to drop into the client's site without consultant rewriting.

nodes.label: ABTestGenerator
nodes.purpose: Future system that writes test definitions to Optimizely / VWO / Convert / Adobe Target — extends AnalyticsProviderAdapter from read to write.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would extend AnalyticsProviderAdapter from read-only ingestion to write-direction test creation
Targets Optimizely, VWO, Convert, Adobe Target
Named as a future interface in §01 system identity and §17 context preservation
outputs: Provider-native test definition created via API write call

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Removes a major manual step in the audit-to-experiment pipeline, turning Neural deliverables directly into running tests.
nodes.example: Future: a finding's variation hypothesis is published as an Optimizely Web experiment with traffic allocation, audiences, and goal events pre-configured.

nodes.label: DesignRecommender
nodes.purpose: Future system producing Figma plugin output + design mockup generation — visual variations to complement code variations.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would emit Figma plugin payloads + generated design mockups for findings
Pairs with FixGenerator (RM1) so designers and engineers receive parallel artifacts
Named as a future interface in §01 system identity and §17 context preservation

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Gives design teams a starting artifact aligned with the same finding the engineers receive, accelerating cross-team adoption.
nodes.example: Future: a finding about hero-section hierarchy yields a Figma frame proposing a redesigned headline + image stack ready for design review.

nodes.label: Design Token Extractor
nodes.purpose: Future system that crawls a site to extract typography / color / spacing tokens, feeding DesignRecommender for on-brand mockups.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would crawl pages to detect font families/sizes, color palettes, spacing scales, and component primitives
Outputs structured design tokens consumed by DesignRecommender (RM3)

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Without tokens, design recommendations look generic; with them, mockups feel native to the client's existing brand system.
nodes.example: Future: extractor crawls client.com and emits { font.heading: 'Inter 600', color.primary: '#FF6600', space.unit: 8 } feeding RM3.

nodes.label: Heatmap Quantitative Layer
nodes.purpose: Future extension of analytics ingestion — native heatmap visualization + finding correlation, beyond raw ingestion in §30.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would add native heatmap visualizations and overlay them against findings to corroborate UX hypotheses with quantitative behavior
Extends §30 analytics ingestion conceptually from data pull to data interpretation
inputs: Heatmap data from analytics providers (Hotjar/Microsoft Clarity/etc.)
Grounded findings with on-page coordinates / selectors
outputs: Heatmap visualization linked to specific findings

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Pairs qualitative heuristic findings with quantitative attention/click data, strengthening the consultant's case to clients.
nodes.example: Future: a finding 'CTA invisible below fold' is overlaid on a Hotjar scroll-depth heatmap showing 35% bounce above the CTA, making the case data-backed.

nodes.label: External Heuristic Scraper
nodes.purpose: Future legal scraper of public CRO research, feeding §22 Heuristic Evolution Phase 4 calibration + Learning Service.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would legally scrape public CRO research (Baymard public reports, Nielsen Norman articles, academic papers) for new heuristic candidates
Feeds §22 Heuristic Evolution Phase 4 calibration pipeline + §28 Learning Service
inputs: Curated list of public CRO research sources

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Keeps the heuristic library current with the broader CRO research ecosystem instead of relying solely on internal authoring.
nodes.example: Future: a Baymard public-report excerpt about checkout friction is auto-imported as a draft heuristic candidate, queued for §22 calibration.

nodes.label: JS Framework Variation Adapters
nodes.purpose: Future per-framework code-gen subset of FixGenerator — React, Vue, Svelte, Angular variants of a single variation.
what_happens: Roadmap concept — no implementation spec yet
Would emit framework-native variation code (React JSX, Vue SFC, Svelte component, Angular component) from a single FixGenerator output
Subset of FixGenerator (RM1) focused on framework-specific code generation
outputs: Framework-native variation code in selected framework

internal_architecture.summary: Post-Master roadmap. No spec yet.
internal_flow: Awaits spec authoring

nodes.why_matters: Most clients live in one framework — handing them React/Vue/Svelte/Angular code instead of generic HTML cuts integration time.
nodes.example: Future: a finding's FixGenerator output is rendered as a React component for a client on Next.js, ready to drop into their codebase.
